title: What if I already filed my tax return before I got my 1095-A, or before I got a corrected or voided one?
meta-description: You may have to file an amended federal income tax return. This means you’ll have to file a corrected version of your return with the IRS.
bite: You may have to file an amended federal income tax return. This means you’ll have to file a corrected version of your return with the IRS.
seo-keywords: amended federal income tax return file an amended tax return corrected tax return tax filing errors
page_name: What if I already filed my tax return before I got my 1095-A, or before I got a corrected or voided one?
page_topic: What if I already filed my tax return before I got my 1095-A, or before I got a corrected or voided one?
excerpt: You may have to file an amended federal income tax return. This means you’ll have to file a corrected version of your return with the IRS.
content:
You may have to file an amended federal income tax return. This means you’ll have to file a corrected version of your return with the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).

You must file an amended return if you get a voided Form 1095-A.

If you don't amend your return, the IRS may contact you about additional tax due.

You may also need to file an amended return if:

  • You filed your tax return before you got a Form 1095-A.
  • You got a corrected Form 1095-A but you used information from the previous Form 1095-A to file your return.

In either case, you’d need to file an amended return only if the information on the Form 1095-A or the corrected 1095-A is different from what you used to fill out Form 8962. If it’s different, you should file an amended return.

Get more information from the IRS about filing an amended return.
